channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta replaces fact-checking with X-style community notes
item.description: Facebook and Instagram owner Meta said Tuesday it's scrapping its third-party fact-checking program and replacing it with Community Notes written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk's social media platform X. Starting in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Still worried RTX 5000 GPUs don’t have enough VRAM? Nvidia’s secret weapon is powerful AI texture compression, and it’s calmed some of my fears
item.description: Nvidia has a raft of fresh technology to make RTX 5000 graphics cards work better, and it all sounds very impressive, at least on paper.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AMD's decision to make FSR 4 exclusive to its new GPUs is a disappointing compromise in a bid to beat Nvidia - I hope it's worth it
item.description: I've always held criticism for Nvidia over upscaling being made exclusive new GPU lineups, but AMD has just done the same thing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia to Launch Native GeForce Now App on Steam Deck, Boosting Performance and VR Support
item.description: Nvidia's GeForce Now cloud gaming service is set to launch a native app on Valve's Steam Deck later this year, bringing improved performance, ray tracing, and DLSS 3 support to the handheld console.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Santa Clara’s Great America theme park lease could be terminated in three years: new docs
item.description: The lease that enables California's Great America amusement park to operate in Santa Clara might be terminated in about three years.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Try this technique to schedule deep work directly into your calendar
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I’m starting this year with a focus on tactics, not just tools. Timeboxing is the tactic I rely on to plan my 100 weekly waking hours.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: This robot vacuum can also bring you a sandwich, and it might be the greatest gadget we've seen yet at CES
item.description: The SwitchBot K20+ Pro does everything you want a robot vacuum to do, plus several more things you don't.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Battle bots: RPA and agentic AI
item.description: I was recently speaking with a client who stated, “We’ve been doing RPA (robotic process automation) for years. What’s the difference with agentic AI? Should I really care, or is it all part of the GenAI hype?” Valid question.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI redefines customer centricity, beyond language, region for IndiaMART: Nikhil Prabhakar
item.description: In a short span of 28 years, IndiaMART has emerged as India’s chosen B2B marketplace and has increased ease of business while simultaneously creating a connected marketplace for all buyers and suppliers across the country.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIO, come crescere di ruolo e fare carriera nel 2025
item.description: Il CIO evolve da manager dell’IT a leader strategico, guidando la collaborazione tra dipartimenti e l’innovazione aziendale. La sinergia con le linee di business e l’adozione di tecnologie come l’intelligenza artificiale diventano essenziali.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia’s Project DIGITS puts AI supercomputing chips on the desktop
item.description: Nvidia has built its generative AI (genAI) business on delivering massive computing capacity to data centers where it can be used to train and refine large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: With o3 having reached AGI, OpenAI turns its sights toward superintelligence
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reinvigorated discussion of artificial general intelligence (AGI), boldly claiming that his company’s newest model has reached that milestone.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The European political establishment has had enough of Elon Musk
item.description: Elon Musk has never been shy about sharing his opinions to his 211 million followers on X. But for those who follow him—and those who get his posts pushed into their feeds on the app—the past few weeks have been more unusual than most.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Python wins Tiobe language of the year honors
item.description: To the surprise of probably no one, Python has won the Tiobe programming language of the year award for 2024, given to the language with the highest increase in ratings in Tiobe’s index of language popularity during the year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CISA says 'no indication' other agencies impacted by Treasury hack
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) said Monday that there is “no indication” other federal agencies were impacted by the recent breach at the Treasury Department.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 great Amazon Prime benefits you’re probably not using
item.description: Let’s address the elephant in the room: Amazon Prime membership costs aren’t getting any cheaper. My credit card just caught the brunt of the yearly auto-renewal.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: AI revolution drives demand for specialized chips, reshaping global markets
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has rapidly transformed the chip industry since its mainstream arrival over the past two years, driving demand for specialized processors, accelerating design innovation, and reshaping global supply chains and markets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What to know about the Meta glasses the New Orleans attacker used to scout the French Quarter
item.description: The FBI says the New Orleans truck attacker who killed 14 people had previously scouted the French Quarter and recorded video with his Meta smart glasses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Someone needs to make AI easy
item.description: 2024 was a year filled with AI possibilities; 2025 needs to turn those possibilities into realities. For that, we need the cloud companies to stop churning out “primitives” such as large language models (LLMs) and instead give developers solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How tech breakthroughs changed health in 2024
item.description: The health and technology sectors intersected in fresh ways in 2024, marking a transformation largely driven by the increased use of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI still hasn’t released tools to deny data collection
item.description: OpenAI has failed to release the tool to opt-out or customize data collection the company promised to make available by 2025, according to Techcrunch.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Confronting the growing drone threat
item.description: There is a solid rationale for prioritizing safe, secure and controlled RF cyber-based counter-UAS technology as decision makers consider future standards. The post Confronting the growing drone threat first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
